elevator_pitch: Amaze is a permanent, indoor maze entertainment venue housed in retrofitted warehouses. With modular walls, rotating themes, story-driven puzzles, and multi-level exploration, it delivers the thrill of a corn maze all year long at a fraction of the cost of escape rooms.
problem: Seasonal corn mazes only run a few weeks each fall, and escape rooms are expensive, limited to small groups, and require lengthy resets between sessions. Adults and families are desperate for fresh, affordable group entertainment that goes beyond bowling alleys and trampoline parks.
solution: Amaze builds large-scale, indoor, modular mazes inside warehouse spaces. Walls can be rearranged monthly for new layouts, themes rotate quarterly with immersive story elements, and dozens of groups can explore simultaneously. Tap-to-pay entry means no lines, no training, and minimal staffing costs.
target_market: Young adults (18-35), families with children, corporate team-building groups, date-night couples, and bachelor/bachelorette parties in mid-size to large metro areas.
revenue_model: Pay-per-entry pricing under $10 per person for a 1-2 hour experience. Additional revenue from concessions (themed food and drinks), merchandise (branded waffle irons, apparel), premium events (overnight 48-hour extreme mazes, zombie chase nights), private group bookings, and monthly leaderboard competitions.
key_features: Modular walls that reconfigure monthly for fresh layouts and quarterly for full theme overhauls
Multi-dimensional paths with tunnels, stairs, second stories, and hidden secret rooms
Story-driven puzzle elements with RFID key cards, map rubbings, and collectible checkpoints
Frictionless tap-to-pay entry with no reservations, no training, and instant access
competitive_advantage: Unlike escape rooms, Amaze requires no reset time between groups, supports dozens of simultaneous visitors, and costs a fraction of the price. Unlike seasonal corn mazes, it operates year-round indoors. The modular design keeps the experience fresh, driving repeat visits that static entertainment venues cannot match.
market_size_estimate: The U.S. amusement and recreation industry generates over $30 billion annually. Escape rooms alone represent a $1.2 billion segment. With lower price points and higher throughput, a single Amaze location could capture the overlap between escape room, corn maze, and general group entertainment audiences in its metro area, representing a TAM of $2-5 billion for a national chain.
go_to_market: Launch a single flagship location in a mid-size city by leasing an empty warehouse in a high-traffic retail corridor. Start with two difficulty tiers and a simple theme, validate demand with low-cost social media marketing, then expand to monthly theme rotations. Use Minecraft-style virtual prototyping to test maze designs before physical builds.
risks: High upfront capital for warehouse lease, modular wall systems, and buildout before revenue begins
Unproven year-round demand for maze experiences outside of the seasonal corn maze window
Theme and layout freshness requires ongoing creative investment to prevent visitor fatigue
year_one_milestones: Open flagship warehouse location with first themed maze and achieve 10,000 visitors in the first quarter
Launch monthly wall reconfiguration cycle and quarterly theme rotations to drive repeat visits
Introduce premium events (zombie chase nights, 48-hour extreme mazes) and hit $500K in annual revenue
